title: Use of an essentially pure mesenchymal stem cell population of the amniotic membrane of umbilical cord for generating a mammalian stem cell carrying a transgene
abstract: The present invention relates to a method of generating a transgenic mesenchymal stem cell population carrying a transgene, a transgenic mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) population obtained by or obtainable by means of enzyme mediated integration, a pharmaceutical composition and uses of the transgenic MSC population in particular for gene therapy. Also claimed are methods of treating a disease or disorder comprising administering the transgenic MSC population or a pharmaceutical composition thereof to a subject in need. In one example, the transgenic MSC population is obtained by zinc-finger (ZFN)-mediated insertion of factor VIII DNA into the AAVS1 locus of MSCs isolated from the amniotic membrane of the umbilical cord.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method of generating a transgenic mesenchymal stem cell population, the method comprising inserting a transgene into the genome of the mesenchymal stem cell population by means of enzyme mediated integration, wherein the mesenchymal stem cell population is an isolated mesenchymal stem cell population of the amniotic membrane of the umbilical cord, wherein at least about 90 % or more cells of the mesenchymal stem cell population express each of the following markers: CD73, CD90 and CD105. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the enzyme mediated integration for inserting the transgene into the genome of the mesenchymal stem cell population is carried out by means of an enzyme selected from the group consisting of a recombinase, a transcription-activator like effector nuclease and a nuclease. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the recombinase is selected from the group consisting of a tyrosine recombinase (YR) and a serine recombinase (SR) and a mixtures thereof. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the tyrosine recombinase is selected from the group consisting of Cre (Cre recombinase-mediated integration), Dre (Dre recombinase-mediated integration) and Flp or wherein the serine recombinase is selected from the group consisting of recombinase phiC3 l (phiC3 l integrase-mediated integration), Bxbl and R4 recombinase-mediated integration. 5. The method of claim 2, wherein the nuclease is selected from the group consisting of zinc finger nuclease (ZFN) (zinc finger nuclease mediated integration), or Cas9 (CRISPR/Cas9-mediated integration). 6. The method of any of claims 1 to 5, wherein least about 90 % or more cells of the isolated mesenchymal stem cell population lack expression of the following markers: CD34, CD45 and HLA-DR. 7. The method of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein at least about 91 % or more, about 92 % or more, about 92 % or more, about 93 % or more, about 94 % or more, about 95 % or more, about 96 % or more, about 97 % or more, about 98 % or more about 99 % or more cells of the isolated mesenchymal stem cell population express each of CD73, CD90 and CD105 and lack expression of each of CD34, CD45 and HLA-DR. 8. The method of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the mesenchymal stem cell population is selected from the group consisting of a human stem cell population, a canine stem cell population, a feline stem cell population, a murine stem cell population, a rat stem cell population, a porcine stem cell population, an equine stem cell population, a stem cell population of an ape, or a stem cell population of a macaque. 9. The method of any of claims 1 to 8, wherein the transgene is integrated into the AAVS 1 locus (present on human chromosome 19 ql3.3-qter) or into Intron 7 of DLC1 in locus 8p22 (present on human chromosome 8) of the human mesenchymal stem cell population. 10. The method of any of the claims 5 to 9, wherein the method comprises inserting the transgene by means of a mutated zinc finger nuclease or by means of Cas9 (CRISPR/Cas9- mediated integration. 11. The method of any of the foregoing claims, wherein the transgene is selected from the group of nucleic acid molecule (gene) encoding a blood coagulation factor and a nucleic acid molecule (gene) encoding a protein hormone secreted by an endocrine gland. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the blood coagulation factor is selected from the group consisting of factor VII, factor VIII and factor IX. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein a deficiency of the expression or secretion of the protein hormone secreted by an endocrine gland is associated with an endocrine deficiency. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the deficiency of the protein hormone is associated with an endocrine deficiency selected from the group consisting of insulin deficiency, Diabetes mellitus associated with insulin deficiency, testosterone deficiency, anemia, hypoglycemia, hyperglycemia, pancreatic deficiency, adrenal deficiency, and thyroid abnormality. 15. The method of claim 12 or 13, wherein the transgene is a nucleic acid molecule encoding a chimeric factor VIII polypeptide. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the transgene encodes a chimeric factor VIII polypeptide comprising human and porcine domains. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the transgene encodes a chimeric factor VIII polypeptide comprising or consisting of porcine Al and A3 domains, human signal peptide, the human A2 domain, a residual human B domain and human C 1 and C2 domains and/or wherein the transgene comprises a sequence of SEQ ID NO. 3. 18. A transgenic mesenchymal stem cell population carrying a transgene obtainable or obtained by a method as defined in any of claim 1 to 17. 19. The transgenic mesenchymal stem cell population of claim 18, wherein the mesenchymal stem cell population is selected from the group consisting of a human stem cell population, a canine stem cell population, a feline stem cell population, an equine stem cell population, a murine stem cell population, a rat stem cell population, a porcine stem cell population, a stem cell population of an ape, or a stem cell population of a macaque. 20. The transgenic mesenchymal stem cell population of claim 18 or 19, wherein the transgene is integrated into the AAVS 1 locus (present on human chromosome 19 ql3.3- qter) of the human mesenchymal stem cell population or into Intron 7 of DLC 1 of the locus 8p22 (present on human chromosome 8). 21. The transgenic mesenchymal stem cell population of any one of claims 18 to 20, wherein least about 81 % or more cells of the transgenic mesenchymal stem cell population express each of CD73, CD90 and CD105, while lacking expression of each of the following markers: CD34, CD45 and HLA-DR. 22. The transgenic mesenchymal stem cell population of claim 21, wherein at least about about 81 % or more, about 82 % or more, at least 83 % or more, at least 84% or more, at least about 85 % or, about 86 % or more, about 87 % or more, about 88 % or more, about 89 % or more, about 90% or more, 91 % or more, about 92 % or more, about 92 % or more, about 93 % or more, about 94 % or more, about 95 % or more, about 96 % or more, about 97 % or more, about 98 % or more about 99 % or more cells of the isolated mesenchymal stem cell population express each of CD73, CD90 and CD105 and lack expression of each of CD34, CD45 and HLA-DR. 23. The transgenic mesenchymal stem cell population of any one of claims 18 to 22, wherein the transgene is a gene encoding a chimeric factor VIII polypeptide. 24. The use of a transgenic mesenchymal stem cell population as defined in any of claims 18 to 23 for the manufacture of a pharmaceutical composition for treating a disease. 25. The use of claim 24, wherein the disease is a disease associated with a deficiency of a gene or deficiency of the expression of the gene, wherein the gene is selected from the group consisting of a gene encoding a blood coagulation factor and a gene encoding a protein hormone secreted by an endocrine gland. 26. The use of claim 25, wherein the blood coagulation factor is selected from the group consisting of factor VII, factor VIII and factor IX. 27. The use of any one of claims 24 to 26, wherein the disease is hemophilia. 28. The use of claim 27, wherein hemophilia is selected from the group consisting of hemophilia A, hemophilia B and hemophilia C. 29. The use of claim 24, wherein the disease is associated with an endocrine deficiency. 30. The use of claim 29, wherein the disease is associated with a deficiency of the expression or secretion of the protein hormone secreted by an endocrine gland. 31. The use of claim 30, wherein the deficiency of the protein hormone is associated with an endocrine deficiency selected from the group consisting of insulin deficiency, diabetes mellitus associated with insulin deficiency, testosterone deficiency, anemia, hypoglycemia, hyperglycemia, pancreatic deficiency, adrenal deficiency, and thyroid abnormality. 32. The use of any of claims 24 to 31, wherein the transgenic mesenchymal stem cell population is to be administered by implantation or injection. 33. The use of claim 32, wherein the transgenic mesenchymal stem cell population is to be implanted subcutaneously. 34. A pharmaceutical composition containing a transgenic mesenchymal stem cell population as defined in any of claims 18 to 23. 35. Use of a transgenic mesenchymal stem cell population as defined in any of claims 18 to 23 for the manufacturing of a pharmaceutical composition for gene therapy treatment. 36. The in vitro use of a mesenchymal stem cell population of the amniotic membrane of the umbilical cord, wherein at least about 90 % or more cells of the mesenchymal stem cell population express each of the following markers: CD73, CD90 and CD 105 for generating 

 a transgenic transgenic mesenchymal stem cell population as defined in any of claims 18 to 23. 37. The use of claim 36, wherein at least about 90 % or more cells of the mesenchymal stem cell population lack expression of the following markers: CD34, CD45 and HLA-DR. 38. The use of any of claims 36 and 37, wherein at least about 91 % or more, about 92 % or more, about 92 % or more, about 93 % or more, about 94 % or more, about 95 % or more, about 96 % or more, about 97 % or more, about 98 % or more about 99 % or more cells of the isolated mesenchymal stem cell population express each of CD73, CD90 and CD 105 and lack expression of each of CD34, CD45 and HLA-DR.
